英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen於1275年出生在蘇格蘭吉德布萊次爾時，他的父親Richard de le Glenn26歲，母親Alicia de Molla26歲。他於大約1292年與Lady Marjory de Mar在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安哈丁頓結婚。他於1244年登記服兵役。他去世於1320年在英國蘇格蘭享年45歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利。

英国苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen于1275年出生在苏格兰吉德布萊次爾时，他的父亲Richard de le Glenn26岁，母亲Alicia de Molla26岁。他于大约1292年与Lady Marjory de Mar在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安哈丁頓结婚。他于1244年登记服兵役。他去世于1320年在英国苏格兰享年45岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Glen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (central Scotland): habitational name from Glen in Traquair (Peebleshire). The placename is derived from Gaelic gleann ‘glen, valley’.
English: habitational name from a place so called in Leicestershire, so named from an Old English word glean ‘glen, valley’ (from Celtic glinn).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish names.

nameMeanings.name: John,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: North American: from the title originally adopted as a nickname parallel to Duke , King , etc. The title was used in England in Norman times as an equivalent of the French comte ‘count’ it is from Old English eorl ‘warrior, nobleman, prince’. In some cases the given name may have been taken from the surname Earl, which was originally either a nickname or a term denoting someone who worked in the household of an earl.

variants.name: Earle
meanings: North American: from the title originally adopted as a nickname parallel to Duke , King , etc. The title was used in England in Norman times as an equivalent of the French comte ‘count’ it is from Old English eorl ‘warrior, nobleman, prince’. In some cases the given name may have been taken from the surname Earl, which was originally either a nickname or a term denoting someone who worked in the household of an earl.

variants.name: Erle
meanings: Variant spelling of   Earl .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen was born in 1275, in Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, his father, Richard de le Glenn, was 26 and his mother, Alicia de Molla, was 26. He married Lady Marjory de Mar about 1292, in Haddington, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom. He registered for military service in 1244. He died in 1320, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 45, and was buried in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen est né en 1275, à Kirkcudbrightshire, Écosse, son père, Richard de le Glenn, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alicia de Molla, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Lady Marjory de Mar environ 1292, à Haddington, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1244. Il est décédé en 1320, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen 1275 in Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de le Glenn 26 und seine Mutter Alicia de Molla 26. Er hat um 1292 in Haddington, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom Lady Marjory de Mar geheiratet. Er hat sich 1244 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1320 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen è nato il nel 1275 nel Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Richard de le Glenn, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Alicia de Molla, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Lady Marjory de Mar circa 1292, nel Haddington, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1244. Morì nel 1320, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolto a Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glenが1275年にスコットランドキルククウドブリトシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard de le Glennは26歳，母Alicia de Mollaは26歳であった。約1292年にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンハッドイントンでLady Marjory de Marと結婚した。彼は1244年に兵役登録をした。彼は1320年にイギリススコットランドで45歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen(이)가 1275년 스컷랜드 커크쿠드브라이츠히르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de le Glenn(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia de Molla(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1292년 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 하딩턴에서 Lady Marjory de Mar(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1244년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1320년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen nasceu em 1275, em Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, seu pai, Richard de le Glenn, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Alicia de Molla, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Marjory de Mar aproximadamente 1292, em Haddington, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1244. Ele faleceu em 1320, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 45 anos, e foi sepultado em Paisley, Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen родился в 1275 г. в местности кирккудбраицхир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Richard de le Glenn было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alicia de Molla, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Marjory de Mar около 1292 г. в местности хаддингтон, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания. В 1244 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1320 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John, Earl of Balmuto De la Glen nació en 1275, en Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, su papá, Richard de le Glenn, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Alicia de Molla, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Lady Marjory de Mar aproximadamente 1292, en Haddington, East Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1244. Murió en 1320, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultado en Paisley, Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
